name: CLEAR CELL SARCOMA OF KIDNEY
definition: BONE-METASTASIZING RENAL TUMOR OF CHILDHOOD rare highly malignant mesenchymal renal neoplasm of childhood with propensity for bone

demographics.Incidence: 20 cases annually in USA; 4-5% of primary renal neoplasms in childhood; 2nd most
demographics.Path: always unilateral unicentric soft well-circumscribed mass replacing most of the kidney /
demographics.Histo: composed of well-defined polygonal to stellate cells with vacuolization, ovoid to

discriminating_features: expansile well-demarcated mass (8-16 cm) with dominant soft-tissue component
cystic component of varying size (few mm to 5 cm) + multiplicity (58%)

general: expansile well-demarcated mass (8-16 cm) with dominant soft-tissue component
cystic component of varying size (few mm to 5 cm) + multiplicity (58%)
lytic / sclerotic / mixed metastatic bone lesions
US: inhomogeneous renal mass of soft-tissue density
mass with fluid-filled anechoic cystic spaces
large fluid-filled spaces + echogenic septa may predominate
CT: heterogeneous enhancement less than that of normal renal parenchyma
MR: low to intermediate signal intensity on T1WI
NUC: bone scan recommended after diagnosis is established

clinical.Prognosis: 60-70% long-term survival rate; aggressive behavior (worse than Wilms tumor)
